 {"id":"bd-x9lqw","title":"feature(pane-terminal): terminal runtime adapter for resizable panes","description":"Goal\n- Deliver full pane interaction in native terminal runtime with inline and alt-screen correctness.\n\nWhat this feature owns\n- Mouse capture policy and routing in terminal runtime.\n- Cell-coordinate drag behavior, keyboard resize parity, focus transitions.\n- Inline scrollback-safe defaults and explicit capture toggles.\n- Robust handling for tmux/screen/terminal capability variation.\n\nDefinition of done\n- Drag + resize is stable under PTY stress tests and resize storms.\n- No terminal cleanup regressions; one-writer invariants preserved.","design":"Terminal adapter converts crossterm + terminal capability variance into canonical pane interaction semantics without breaking inline/alt-screen safety.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Terminal pane interactions (mouse + keyboard) are fully implemented with robust lifecycle cleanup.\n2) Unit/integration tests cover capability variance and edge interruptions.\n3) PTY E2E scenarios validate user workflows in inline and alt-screen modes.\n4) Diagnostic logs clearly capture event routing, geometry decisions, and failure context.","notes":"Execution lane: Track B (terminal adapter).\nPrimary sequence: bd-2axgy -> bd-2ljzu -> bd-3jmx7 -> {bd-6u66i, bd-24v9m}.\nUser impact: low-latency, stable terminal resizing with safe cleanup in inline and alt-screen modes.\nEvidence required: terminal unit/integration tests + PTY E2E + event-routing diagnostics.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":960,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:30:48.808214210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:46:50.612476294Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crossterm","inline","pane","terminal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x9lqw","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkzq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:31:09.235422787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-x9lqw","depends_on_id":"bd-2mgle","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:31:09.588487245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-x9lqw","depends_on_id":"bd-gajnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:31:05.995373031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-xcca9","title":"test(runtime): raise undo/command.rs coverage from 77% toward 85%","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:11:23.077080995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:15:23.504335367Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:15:23.504303347Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1161,"issue_id":"bd-xcca9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkElk picking this up.  {"id":"bd-xh8s","title":"Inline scroll-region + cursor safety for log writes","description":"Problem: Bottom-anchored inline mode can save or restore cursor inside the UI region; log writes may overwrite UI if the cursor is not moved to the log region before writing. Scroll-region boundaries might be incorrect.

Scope:
- Ensure log writes always move the cursor to the log region before writing, regardless of prior save or restore.
- Validate and normalize scroll-region boundaries for both top- and bottom-anchored inline strategies.
- Make cursor save or restore deterministic and never leave the UI region active during log writes.

Testing:
- Unit tests for TerminalWriter log writes in inline top and bottom anchors; assert cursor position and scroll region.
- E2E script streaming logs while UI updates; assert UI stability and no overwrites; include PTY capture and hex dump on failure.
- Additional test verifying scroll-region is reset or cleared on exit.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Log writes never corrupt UI across anchors.
- Scroll-region logic is correct and verified by tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:39.481234591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:40:51.272912449Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:40:51.272894154Z","close_reason":"Implemented cursor safety for log writes in inline mode","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-xi1ii","title":"P0: Verify and harden VS16 emoji width handling — close audit gap","description":"## Context

A recent audit of the MCP Agent Mail TUI incorrectly reported that "VS16/FE0F emoji width stripping was never implemented in frankentui." Investigation reveals this is **FALSE** — the fix IS implemented:

- `strip_vs16()` at `crates/ftui-core/src/lib.rs:138-147` strips U+FE0F before width calculation
- `grapheme_width()` at lines 224-234 applies the stripping when `trust_vs16_width()` is false
- `trust_vs16_width()` at lines 105-119 defaults to false (terminal-realistic mode)
- Comprehensive tests exist in `crates/ftui-text/tests/unicode_width_corpus.rs` (lines 236-279, 583-612)
- `FTUI_EMOJI_VS16_WIDTH=unicode` opt-in for terminals that correctly render VS16 as width 2

## Task

The audit false negative means there is a **documentation/discoverability gap** and potentially untested edge cases. This bead ensures:

### 1. Document VS16 behavior in crate-level docs
Add a `# Emoji Width Handling` section to ftui-core rustdoc explaining:
- Default behavior (strip VS16, render text-default emoji at width 1)
- Opt-in for modern terminals
- Which emoji are affected (text-default + VS16 like ⚙️, 🖼️, ⚡️, ❤️)

### 2. Expand test coverage for edge cases
Add tests for:
- **Keycap sequences**: `#️⃣` (# + VS16 + U+20E3) — should be width 1
- **Flag sequences**: 🇺🇸 (two regional indicators) — should be width 2
- **ZWJ sequences with VS16**: 👨‍👩‍👧 family emoji — should be width 2
- **Skin tone modifiers + VS16**: ✌🏽️ (victory hand + skin tone + VS16)
- **Multiple VS16 in one grapheme cluster** (malformed but should not panic)
- **Empty string after VS16 strip** (degenerate: lone U+FE0F)

### 3. Verify no width cache invalidation issues
The `WidthCache` in `crates/ftui-text/src/width_cache.rs` caches computed widths. Verify that:
- Cache keys include the trust_vs16_width policy
- Changing FTUI_EMOJI_VS16_WIDTH at runtime does not return stale cached widths
- (Since it uses OnceLock, the env var is read once — document this behavior)

## Key Files
- `crates/ftui-core/src/lib.rs` — lines 105-147, 211-236
- `crates/ftui-text/tests/unicode_width_corpus.rs` — lines 236-279, 583-612
- `crates/ftui-text/src/width_cache.rs` — cache implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All new edge case tests pass
- Rustdoc section added to ftui-core
- Cache behavior documented
- Zero regressions in existing 600+ line unicode width test corpus","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:01:26.551534079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:01:26.551534079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xn2","title":"Create CI/CD pipeline configuration","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - CI/CD PIPELINE

## Background
Continuous integration ensures code quality, test coverage, and performance budgets are maintained. Must run on every PR and push to main.

## Technical Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

#### Jobs Overview
| Job | Trigger | Purpose | Blocking |
|-----|---------|---------|----------|
| check | PR, push | Format, clippy, tests | Yes |
| coverage | PR, push | Coverage thresholds | Yes |
| benchmarks | push to main | Performance budgets | Warn |
| e2e | PR, push | End-to-end tests | Yes |
| docs | PR, push | Doc build verification | Yes |

#### Check Job
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --no-default-features

#### Coverage Job
- Use cargo-llvm-cov for coverage
- Thresholds:
  - Overall: ≥ 70%
  - ftui-core: ≥ 80%
  - ftui-render: ≥ 75%
- Upload to codecov.io

#### Benchmarks Job
- Run on push to main only
- Performance budgets from plan:
  - Cell comparison: < 1ns
  - Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
  - Diff scan: < 100µs
  - Layout solve (20 items): < 100µs
- Fail on 2x regression

#### E2E Job
- Run scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Upload artifacts on failure
- Test on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest

#### Docs Job
- cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
- Verify no broken links
- Upload docs artifact

### Matrix Testing
- OS: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Rust: stable, nightly
- Features: default, all, minimal

### Caching
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache ~/.cargo/git
- Cache target/ with hash key

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check job with format/clippy/tests
- [ ] coverage job with thresholds
- [ ] benchmarks job with budgets
- [ ] e2e job with script execution
- [ ] docs job with verification
- [ ] Matrix: 3 OS × 2 toolchains
- [ ] Proper caching configuration
- [ ] Badge in README

 {"id":"bd-xox","title":"Epic: Observability & Evidence - Logging, Tracing, Metrics, Evidence Ledger","description":"Structured observability across FrankenTUI: tracing spans with structured fields for every diff/layout/render cycle, log levels (TRACE for frame internals, DEBUG for strategy selection, INFO for lifecycle events, WARN for frame budget overruns, ERROR for terminal protocol violations), Prometheus-compatible metrics (frame_time_histogram, diff_strategy_selected, layout_recompute_count, widget_render_duration), evidence ledger integration for Bayesian strategy decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:56.196737803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:56.196737803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","metrics","observability","tracing"]}
 {"id":"bd-xox.1","title":"Structured tracing span hierarchy for render pipeline","description":"Define and enforce canonical span hierarchy: terminal.init -> runtime.update -> runtime.view -> layout.compute -> widget.render -> diff.strategy_select -> ansi.present -> render.sync_bracket -> render.frame. Every span carries: frame_number, timestamp_us, thread_id. Parent-child relationships enforced. Span naming convention: component.operation (lowercase, dot-separated). TRACING: this IS the tracing specification. All spans defined here are the source of truth. LOG: ERROR if span hierarchy violated (e.g., widget.render without parent runtime.view). METRICS: histogram span_duration_us{span_name=...}.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:33.125594793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:47.792620226Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:47.792593225Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: comprehensive tracing spans across runtime (ftui.program.init/update/view) and render (diff_compute, emit_diff, presenter)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hierarchy","observability","spans","tracing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xox.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-xox.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
